steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage uses present tense throughout — 'is a business,' 'stands in contrast,' and 'are shared' all use present forms. The blank verb must match that timeframe.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — benefit: simple present tense, consistent with the rest of the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — had benefited: past perfect, shifts the timeframe backward. ✗
  • Choice C — benefited: simple past, contradicts the present-tense passage. ✗
  • Choice D — were benefiting: past progressive, also shifts the timeframe backward. ✗
A is correct.
